applied_optimizations.name: Fused BatchNorm + ReLU Triton kernel
applied_optimizations.description: Implemented triton_bn_relu kernel that fuses BatchNorm and ReLU operations. Handles both training mode (uses batch statistics via F.batch_norm) and eval mode (uses running statistics via Triton kernel).

applied_optimizations.name: Fused Residual Add + ReLU Triton kernel
applied_optimizations.description: Implemented triton_residual_relu kernel that fuses residual addition and ReLU operations.

applied_optimizations.name: Wavefront-aligned BLOCK_SIZE
applied_optimizations.description: Set BLOCK_SIZE to multiples of 64 (AMD MI355X wavefront size) using triton.next_power_of_2 with min 64.

attempted_but_failed.name: torch.compile with mode=default
attempted_but_failed.reason: Triton kernel compilation failures on ROCm - 'failed to legalize operation ttg.async_copy_global_to_local'
attempted_but_failed.error: RuntimeError: PassManager::run failed for Triton convolution kernels

attempted_but_failed.name: BN kernel using running stats in training mode
attempted_but_failed.reason: Correctness failure - test harness runs models in training mode which uses batch statistics, not running statistics

notes: torch.compile fails on ROCm due to Triton kernel issues with convolutions
Main bottlenecks: 3x3 convolutions (29.2%), BatchNorm (15.7%), elementwise ops (14.5%)
Key fix: Triton BN kernel must handle training mode (batch stats) vs eval mode (running stats)
Wavefront size is 64 on MI355X - BLOCK_SIZE should be multiples of 64
Current implementation fuses BN+ReLU and residual+ReLU but convolutions still use MIOpen
Performance is variable - sometimes faster, sometimes slower than reference due to MIOpen autotuning
Next optimization opportunities: optimize convolutions, improve Triton kernel configurations
